sections.title: The Pivot Joint (The Rotation Joint)
points: Definition: Allows one bone to rotate or turn around another bone.
Location 1 (Neck): Between the Atlas (1st vertebra) and the Axis (2nd vertebra). This allows the head to turn left and right.
Location 2 (Forearm): Between the radius and ulna. This allows you to turn your palm up and down.
The Bones: The Axis has a peg-like projection (the dens) that the Atlas turns on.

sections.title: The Gliding Joint (The Sliding Joint)
points: Definition: Two relatively flat surfaces slide or glide over each other.
Function: Provides flexibility for complex movements like writing or walking on uneven ground.
Locations: Found in the Wrists (carpals), Ankles (tarsals), and between the processes of the Vertebrae.

sections.text: Exam Tip: Freely Movable (Synovial) joints like Pivot and Gliding joints MUST have Synovial Fluid and Cartilage to prevent friction. If the fluid dries up, the bones will rub and cause pain!

cards.fact: The main movement of a pivot joint.
cards.mnemonic: A Pivot turns like a Pilot turns a wheel!

cards.fact: Allows bones to slide past each other.
Atlas supports the skull; Axis acts as the turning point.
cards.mnemonic: The Atlas holds the weight; the Axis rotates the gate!
